_read_me: This is the durable, checkable validation-state record referenced by docs/release-validation-plan.md's 'Validation State Tracking' section. Read that section before editing this file by hand. Never hand-wave an entry as validated: every non-null commit/date/validator/evidence_ref below must correspond to a real, completed validation run with real evidence (command output, CI run URL, or a PR/issue comment quoting it) -- see the proposed AG-VAL-028 rule in the docs/release-validation-plan.md PR body. This file is intentionally seeded with 'never validated' placeholders; it must not be pre-filled with a fabricated pass.

last_stack_validation.validator: djdomi-claude (agent-run Stack-validation pass, docs/release-validation-plan.md Part B, against the v0.3.0 release-candidate branch on that commit)
last_stack_validation.scope: Part B scenarios named in the assigned task: bring-up, DNS resolution both modes, DHCP all three modes, cache HIT/MISS (HTTP + SSL/MITM HTTPS), NATS full secondary lifecycle incl. active-disconnect/xkey, Admin UI reachability + watchdog dashboard card, watchdog auto-restart coverage, central logging/syslog forwarding, resource-leak/cleanup pass. Ran against a real deploy/dev stack built from this exact commit's source on a self-hosted Linux runner (lancache-241), plus two delegated sub-agent runs (lancache-243 for the three DHCP simulation scripts, lancache-229 for the NATS simulation script, images built from this same source commit).
last_stack_validation.summary: PASS with real evidence: bring-up (after working around issue #1215, a deploy/dev-only IP-allocation race -- confirmed 3x reproducible, scoped to deploy/dev only, not quickstart/prod/full-setup); DNS resolution both modes (dig + openssl s_client showed real passthrough CDN cert in standard mode, real LanCache-NG CA cert in SSL/MITM mode); cache HIT/MISS for HTTP (both modes share one cache, query-string-invariant key confirmed) and SSL/MITM HTTPS (clean MISS-then-HIT, byte-identical); DHCP Kea/dnsmasq-proxy/dnsmasq-relay all confirmed via their dedicated simulation scripts plus (for relay) a real Admin-UI-driven end-to-end proof with correct rendered dnsmasq config; NATS full lifecycle (registration/isolation/rotation/removal, active-disconnect via connz polling, xkey encryption with positive assertion in this run reusing the prior negative-control proof per the plan's own reuse guidance) via the delegated nats-secondary-auth-callout-simulation.sh run; Admin UI reachability + auth enforcement; watchdog dashboard card live green-to-red-to-green transition via a real container stop; watchdog auto-restart via a real external SIGSTOP on nats, including a live reproduction of the known #1166 cosmetic restart-warning bug (StartedAt confirmed genuinely new despite the spurious WARNING); central logging/syslog forwarding (real nginx access-log lines, matching earlier cache-test requests, confirmed arriving at the syslog-ng target via fluent-bit); resource-leak/cleanup pass (docker ps -a / docker volume ls both empty for all lancache-prefixed items after `down -v`, no stale connections observed). PARTIAL, correctly left unmarked in subsystem_validation below rather than blanket-passed: Admin UI cache-resize (PR #1174) -- confirmed the UI submission persists correctly and confirmed `lancache-converge.service` is actually two separate ExecStart steps (`setup.sh converge-reconcile` merges the override into .env; a separate, distinct step actually runs `docker compose up -d`), but did not run the second step, so nginx's rendered `max_size` was never confirmed to change -- the check's own headline pass/fail criterion remains unproven this pass. New findings filed as issues, not fixed in this pass (out of scope): #1215 (deploy/dev IP-race, reproduced 3x) and #1216 (setup.sh's converge-reconcile DHCP-mode accept-list wasn't updated for dnsmasq-relay -- a real, narrow gap confirmed by reading the code; its downstream impact on profile self-heal is analytically derived, not reproduced against a live systemd timer). Also fixed two real inaccuracies in docs/release-validation-plan.md itself found while dogfooding it (standard-mode HTTPS does not cache at all -- corrected from a wrong prior claim; and an image-tag-staleness trap -- ghcr.io `nightly` was confirmed 29 commits behind this exact commit, missing every 2026-07-24 feature). IMPORTANT CAVEAT: the v0.3.0 branch received one additional commit after this pass began (an AGENTS.md governance addition, PR #1211) -- per this file's own full_invalidation_triggers, an AGENTS.md change is a full-invalidation trigger; this record's commit field intentionally names the exact commit actually tested (88ddbf6a), which is confirmed still an ancestor of v0.3.0's current tip, but a fresh (at minimum incremental) validation pass should be run against v0.3.0's current tip before treating this record as covering it for a release decision.
last_stack_validation.evidence_ref: https://github.com/wiki-mod/lancache-ng/pull/1217 (this record's own introducing PR -- see its description for the full per-scenario evidence log); see also issues #1215 and #1216 for the two new findings

recertified.note: Resolves this record's own IMPORTANT CAVEAT above. The only diff on v0.3.0 between the tested commit (88ddbf6a) and this commit is AG-WF-026 (PR #1211) -- an AGENTS.md governance/process rule addition with no code or runtime-behavior change. No re-test was performed. This recertification was reviewed and approved by both the AI agent (djdomi-claude) and the maintainer (djdomi) based on the diff's actual content, not a blanket exemption from full_invalidation_triggers -- any future v0.3.0 commit that touches code, config, or a non-doc-only AGENTS.md change still requires a real re-validation pass before this record can be trusted again.
recertified.evidence_ref: this file's own edit, cross-referenced from issue/PR discussion; see PR #1211 for the recertified diff

last_ci_validation.validator: claude-agent (Part A CI validation pass, real Linux self-hosted runner host)
last_ci_validation.summary: Real Part A (CI) pass against the frozen v0.3.0 branch tip (identical content to current_dev at this commit): cargo fmt/check(-D warnings)/clippy(-D warnings)/test PASS for all three crates (ui 353 tests, dns/nats-subscriber 49 tests, pxe-client-probe 4 tests) on real Linux via the same rustup-stable toolchain CI itself installs. dhcp-relay-flow-simulation.sh PASS (real two-segment DORA relay proof). nats-secondary-auth-callout-simulation.sh run twice against locally-built images pinned to this exact commit (ghcr.io nightly was stale/behind at run time): positive control PASS (connz-polled active-disconnect #681, xkv1 encryption marker #682 with no plaintext JWT marker), negative control (xkey removed) correctly FAILED with the expected plaintext-marker error. check-vex-drift.sh PASS + negative control (mutated .trivyignore.yaml) correctly failed with a real diff. setup_update_idempotence.bats guard PASS + negative control (NTP_ENABLED removed) correctly failed naming that key. shellcheck: 0 findings. bats tests/bats: 714/715 pass -- the 1 real failure (check-bats-path-filter-coverage.sh's own self-test) was root-caused to a genuine gap (dnsmasq-relay.conf.template missing from build-tools.yml's path filters) and fixed in PR #1209, re-verified there (0 gaps after fix). shellspec: 3/3 pass. actionlint (default invocation, matching CI's own exact 'Run ShellCheck and ActionLint' step): FAILED to complete -- hung indefinitely, reproduced 3/3 times at this commit; corroborated by a real, independently-stuck CI job (shellcheck job on PR feat/681-nats-active-disconnect, in_progress 5+ hours using the actual published ghcr.io build-tools image, not a local-build artifact). Disabling the embedded shellcheck integration (-shellcheck=) completes in 0.017s with 0 findings, so the workflow YAML content itself is clean; the check as CI currently invokes it is not reliably completing. UPDATE (maintainer follow-up, same day): after this hang was flagged, the maintainer audited the rest of the run queue and found 7 more runs stuck the identical way (in_progress for hours at the same step, several on already-merged/deleted branches), all cancelled -- confirming this is a systemic slot-leak, not a one-off, and that a meaningful share of what looked like general runner-pool congestion during this validation pass was actually these zombie runs never releasing their slots. See PR #1213's comments for the full list of affected runs. Not exercised in this pass (explicitly deferred, not silently assumed passing): DHCP-Kea and dnsmasq-ProxyDHCP live E2E simulations (dhcp-kea-lease-flow-simulation, dhcp-proxy-pxe-simulation), DNS live dig-based resolution and setup-reset-dns-config-simulation.sh, check-workflow-service-lists.sh, Admin UI/watchdog live Part B stack checks (out of Part A CI scope by design), governance-doc manual review beyond the plan-doc fix itself.
last_ci_validation.evidence_ref: https://github.com/wiki-mod/lancache-ng/pull/1213 (full command transcript in the PR description and first comment); https://github.com/wiki-mod/lancache-ng/pull/1209 (ci-build-tools path-filter-coverage gap: before/after command output)

subsystem_validation._note: path_prefixes mirror scripts/classify-image-impact.sh's real touches_prefix() rules (the same classifier build-push.yml's detect-changes job and the promote job's version-bump logic both already consume) so staleness can be computed the same way CI already reasons about relevance, instead of a second, drifting definition. A subsystem entry is stale the moment `git log <its last commit>..HEAD -- <its path_prefixes>` is non-empty, OR whenever AGENTS.md/.github/AGENTS.md changed (see 'full_invalidation_triggers' below), OR whenever the recorded commit itself predates last_stack_validation/last_ci_validation's own commit for that layer.

dhcp-kea.validator: djdomi-claude (delegated sub-agent run on lancache-243, dhcp-kea-lease-flow-simulation.sh, built from this commit's source)
dhcp-kea.evidence_ref: https://github.com/wiki-mod/lancache-ng/pull/1217; superseded, chronologically later than an earlier CI-layer (Part A) pass of the same subsystem recorded in full under last_ci_validation.summary above and https://github.com/wiki-mod/lancache-ng/pull/1213 (dhcp_kea_config_generation.bats, dhcp_lease_flow_parsing.bats -- both PASS, part of the 714/715 bats run)

dhcp-dnsmasq.validator: djdomi-claude (dhcp-proxy-pxe-simulation.sh + dhcp-relay-flow-simulation.sh via delegated sub-agent on lancache-243, both built from this commit's source; dnsmasq-relay additionally confirmed first-hand via a real Admin-UI-driven end-to-end run on lancache-241)
dhcp-dnsmasq.evidence_ref: https://github.com/wiki-mod/lancache-ng/pull/1217; superseded, chronologically later than an earlier CI-layer (Part A) pass of the same subsystem recorded in full under last_ci_validation.summary above and https://github.com/wiki-mod/lancache-ng/pull/1213 (scripts/dhcp-relay-flow-simulation.sh PASS; dhcp_proxy_known_good_snapshot.bats/dhcp_proxy_optional_directives.bats/setup_dhcp_mode.bats all PASS)

dns-pdns.validator: djdomi-claude (first-hand: dig + openssl s_client against a real deploy/dev stack on lancache-241, both DNS instances and both TLS-handling modes)
dns-pdns.evidence_ref: https://github.com/wiki-mod/lancache-ng/pull/1217; superseded, chronologically later than an earlier CI-layer (Part A) pass of the same subsystem recorded in full under last_ci_validation.summary above and https://github.com/wiki-mod/lancache-ng/pull/1213 (dns_zone_generation.bats/dns_known_good_snapshot.bats/dns_config_snapshot_idempotence.bats all PASS; services/dns/nats-subscriber crate's own fmt/check/clippy/test all PASS, 49 tests)

nats.note: NATS has no dedicated classify-image-impact.sh key -- its functional surface spans the ui, setup_runtime, and deploy classifier keys. Treat a change as NATS-relevant if it touches any prefix above, not only a single classifier boolean.
nats.validator: djdomi-claude (delegated sub-agent run on lancache-229, nats-secondary-auth-callout-simulation.sh, images built from this commit's source and locally retagged -- not a first-hand run, taken on the sub-agent's reported command output)
nats.evidence_ref: https://github.com/wiki-mod/lancache-ng/pull/1217; superseded, chronologically later than an earlier CI-layer (Part A) pass of the same subsystem recorded in full under last_ci_validation.summary above and https://github.com/wiki-mod/lancache-ng/pull/1213 (same script run twice against locally-built images: positive control PASS -- credential isolation, rotation invalidation, real connz-polled active disconnect #681, xkv1 marker with no plaintext JWT #682; negative control with xkey removed correctly FAILED with the expected plaintext-marker error)

admin-ui.evidence_ref: https://github.com/wiki-mod/lancache-ng/pull/1213 -- PARTIAL: services/ui crate's own fmt/check(-D warnings)/clippy(-D warnings)/test all pass (353 tests, incl. nats_auth_callout, watchdog_status, dhcp routes, secondary-registration modules), and the NATS simulation above drives real Admin UI HTTP routes (session/CSRF, secondary registration/rotation/removal). Live dashboard/cache-resize Part B checks (nginx -T rendered-config proof, watchdog dashboard green/red transitions) were NOT run -- out of this Part A CI pass's scope by design, not assumed passing.

watchdog.validator: djdomi-claude (first-hand on lancache-241: real container stop for the dashboard-card transition, real external `docker kill --signal=STOP` for auto-restart, including a live reproduction of the known #1166 cosmetic-warning gap)
watchdog.evidence_ref: https://github.com/wiki-mod/lancache-ng/pull/1217; superseded, chronologically later than an earlier CI-layer (Part A) pass of the same subsystem recorded in full under last_ci_validation.summary above and https://github.com/wiki-mod/lancache-ng/pull/1213 (watchdog_test CI job's own checks reproduced directly, PASS; extensive watchdog.sh bats coverage -- maybe_purge, maybe_prune_syslog, check_and_maybe_restart nats, write_status convergence -- all PASS as part of the 714/715 bats run)

proxy-cache.validator: djdomi-claude (first-hand on lancache-241: real HTTP MISS/HIT + query-string-invariance, real SSL/MITM HTTPS MISS/HIT, confirmed standard-mode HTTPS is genuinely uncached blind passthrough)

ci-build-tools.evidence_ref: https://github.com/wiki-mod/lancache-ng/pull/1213 (full transcript) and https://github.com/wiki-mod/lancache-ng/pull/1209 (the fix + before/after check-bats-path-filter-coverage.sh output) -- MIXED result, recorded honestly rather than rounded to a single pass/fail: check-bats-path-filter-coverage.sh FAILED against the unmodified v0.3.0/current_dev tree (2 real gaps, services/dhcp-proxy/dnsmasq-relay.conf.template missing from both path-filter lists) -- fixed in PR #1209, re-verified there (0 gaps after). shellcheck PASSED (0 findings, repo-wide). actionlint FAILED to complete as CI actually invokes it (default embedded-shellcheck invocation hangs indefinitely, reproduced 3/3 times; corroborated by an independently-stuck real CI job using the published ghcr.io image, not a local-build artifact); with the embedded shellcheck integration disabled (-shellcheck=, a deviation from CI's real invocation) it completes in 0.017s with 0 findings, so the workflow YAML content itself is not known to be wrong, but the check as CI runs it is not reliably completing right now. Confirmed systemic same-day: the maintainer found and cancelled 7 more runs stuck identically (some on already-merged/deleted branches), so this was leaking runner slots repo-wide, not an isolated fluke -- see PR #1213's comments. check-workflow-service-lists.sh was NOT run in this pass. bats/shellspec smoke-tests of the build-tools image itself: 714/715 and 3/3 respectively (see nats/dhcp-dnsmasq entries for the one real bats failure, which is this same path-filter gap).

governance-sbom.evidence_ref: https://github.com/wiki-mod/lancache-ng/pull/1213 -- check-vex-drift.sh reports vex.openvex.json in sync with .trivyignore.yaml (PASS); generate-vex.sh | jq empty produces valid JSON; negative control (a scratch copy of .trivyignore.yaml with an injected fake CVE entry) correctly made the drift guard FAIL with a real, readable diff, proving the guard still discriminates a genuine mismatch. AGENTS.md/.github/AGENTS.md unchanged since #1205, so no governance full-invalidation trigger fired in this pass.

setup-runtime.evidence_ref: https://github.com/wiki-mod/lancache-ng/pull/1213 -- tests/bats/setup_update_idempotence.bats's migrate_env_for_update() key-drift guard (first @test) PASSES on a clean checkout; negative control (NTP_ENABLED removed from write_converged_env_fixture() in a scratch copy of the test file) correctly FAILS, naming exactly 'NTP_ENABLED' as the missing key. scripts/dhcp-relay-flow-simulation.sh (also under scripts/) covered under dhcp-dnsmasq above. Other scripts/ entries (check-workflow-service-lists.sh, check-idempotence-test-coverage.sh, etc.) NOT individually re-run in this pass.

full_invalidation_triggers: AGENTS.md or .github/AGENTS.md changed since the recorded commit (governance change can redefine what 'correct' means for every subsystem, not just one)

The diff since the recorded commit could not be reconstructed (force-push discarding the old tip, an unreachable base, or any case classify-image-impact.sh itself would fall back to --all-changed for) -- treat as fully stale rather than guessing
The recorded commit is not an ancestor of the commit under test (a rebase/history-rewrite happened) -- re-derive the diff from a real merge-base, do not assume the old record still applies
